---
package: "openpipeline_qc"
version: "v0.3.0"
title: "generate_qc_report"
namespace: "Workflows"
description: "Run the ingestion QC report generation"
type: "workflow"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline_qc v0.3.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `generate_qc_report`  
Namespace: `workflows`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_qc/tree/v0.3.0/src/workflows/generate_qc_report)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_qc)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_qc/v0.3.0/components/workflows/generate_qc_report)

:::
## Visualisation

```{mermaid}
flowchart TB
    c_correction_cellbender_remove_background["cellbender_remove_background"]
    c_ingestion_qc_generate_html["generate_html"]
    c_ingestion_qc_h5mu_to_qc_json["h5mu_to_qc_json"]
    c_metadata_add_id["add_id"]
    c_move_files_to_directory["move_files_to_directory"]
    c_workflows_qc_qc["qc"]
    c_correction_cellbender_remove_background --> c_workflows_qc_qc
    c_ingestion_qc_h5mu_to_qc_json --> c_ingestion_qc_generate_html
    c_metadata_add_id --> c_correction_cellbender_remove_background
    c_workflows_qc_qc --> c_ingestion_qc_h5mu_to_qc_json
    c_workflows_qc_qc --> c_move_files_to_directory
    style c_correction_cellbender_remove_background fill:#f2f6f3,stroke:#8ba295,stroke-dasharray:5 3,color:#566b5f
    style c_ingestion_qc_generate_html fill:#e2f4e7,stroke:#2b8f52,color:#13201a
    style c_ingestion_qc_h5mu_to_qc_json fill:#e2f4e7,stroke:#2b8f52,color:#13201a
    style c_metadata_add_id fill:#e2f4e7,stroke:#2b8f52,color:#13201a
    style c_move_files_to_directory fill:#e2f4e7,stroke:#2b8f52,color:#13201a
    style c_workflows_qc_qc fill:#e2f4e7,stroke:#2b8f52,color:#13201a
```

*Dashed nodes are optional steps.*


## Argument groups

### Inputs

|Name                       |Description                                                                                                                                                                                     |Attributes                                        |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--id`                     |The sample IDs to include in the report. If not provided, the sample IDs will be extracted from the h5mu files.                                                                                 |`string`, example: `"sample1"`                    |
|`--input`                  |The input h5mu files.                                                                                                                                                                           |`file`, required, example: `"path/to/file1.h5mu"` |
|`--ingestion_method`       |                                                                                                                                                                                                |`string`, required                                |
|`--sample_metadata`        |The sample metadata file corresponding to .obs fields in the h5mu input files, to be used for grouping in the report.                                                                           |`file`, example: `"path/to/file.csv"`             |
|`--max_samples_per_report` |The maximum number of samples to be included per report.  Multiple reports will be generated (with samples equally divided over all reports) if number of input samples exceeds this threshold. |`integer`, default: `20`                          |


### Options

|Name               |Description                                                                                                    |Attributes                                                                                      |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--var_gene_names` |The column name in the .var h5mu files that contains the gene names. If not provided, .var_names will be used. |`string`, example: `"gene_symbol"`                                                              |
|`--obs_metadata`   |The metadata keys in the h5mu .obs to include in the report.                                                   |List of `string`, example: `"donor_id", "cell_type", "batch", "condition"`, multiple_sep: `";"` |


### QC options

|Name                             |Description                                                                        |Attributes                           |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--var_name_mitochondrial_genes` |In which .var slot to store a boolean array corresponding the mitochondrial genes. |`string`, default: `"mitochondrial"` |
|`--var_name_ribosomal_genes`     |In which .var slot to store a boolean array corresponding the ribosomal genes.     |`string`, default: `"ribosomal"`     |
|`--min_total_counts`             |Minimum total counts for a cell to be included in the output.                      |`integer`, default: `10`             |
|`--min_num_nonzero_vars`         |Minimum number of nonzero vars for a cell to be included in the output.            |`integer`, default: `10`             |


### Cellbender options

|Name                  |Description                           |Attributes                  |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--run_cellbender`    |Whether to run cellbender or not.     |`boolean`, default: `FALSE` |
|`--cellbender_epochs` |Number of epochs to train cellbender. |`integer`, default: `150`   |


### Options for CosMx reports

|Name                   |Description                                                                 |Attributes                                                                                  |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--obs_area`           |The key in the h5mu .obs field that contains the cell area.                 |`string`, default: `"Area"`                                                                 |
|`--obs_aspect_ratio`   |The key in the h5mu .obs field that contains the aspect ratio.              |`string`, default: `"AspectRatio"`                                                          |
|`--obs_dapi_stain`     |The keys in the h5mu .obs field that contains the DAPI stain intensity.     |List of `string`, default: `"Mean.DAPI", "Max.DAPI"`, multiple_sep: `";"`                   |
|`--obs_membrane_stain` |The keys in the h5mu .obs field that contains the membrane stain intensity. |List of `string`, default: `"Mean.MembraneStain", "Max.MembraneStain"`, multiple_sep: `";"` |
|`--obs_panck_stain`    |The keys in the h5mu .obs field that contains the PanCK stain intensity.    |List of `string`, default: `"Mean.PanCK", "Max.PanCK"`, multiple_sep: `";"`                 |
|`--obs_cd45_stain`     |The keys in the h5mu .obs field that contains the CD45 stain intensity.     |List of `string`, default: `"Mean.CD45", "Max.CD45"`, multiple_sep: `";"`                   |
|`--obs_cd3_stain`      |The keys in the h5mu .obs field that contains the CD3 stain intensity.      |List of `string`, default: `"Mean.CD3", "Max.CD3"`, multiple_sep: `";"`                     |


### Outputs

|Name                      |Description                                 |Attributes                                                                    |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--output_qc_report`      |The output HTML report                      |List of `file`, required, example: `"path/to/file.html"`, multiple_sep: `";"` |
|`--output_processed_h5mu` |Folder containing the processed h5mu files. |`file`, required, default: `"qc_h5mu"`                                        |

## Authors


  * Jakub Majercik [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/jakubmajercik) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/jakubmajercik) (author)

  * Dorien Roosen [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/dorien-er) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/dorien-roosen) (author)

  * Robrecht Cannoodt [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/rcannood) [{{< fa brands orcid >}}](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-3641-729X) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/robrechtcannoodt) (author)

  * Weiwei Schultz (contributor)

## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_qc \
  -r v0.3.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/generate_qc_report/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_qc/v0.3.0/components/workflows/generate_qc_report).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_qc \
  -r v0.3.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/generate_qc_report/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).
